name: Wolfgang Puck's Pumpkin Pie
recipeIngredient: Fresh ground white pepper, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Line a buttered 10-inch pie dish or flan ring with pastry and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
3. Line the chilled pastry with parchment paper and fill with pie weights or uncooked beans. Bake for 25 minutes, or until the crust is golden. Let cool.
4. Remove the parchment paper and weights from the cooled pastry shell.
5. In a large stainless steel saucepan, combine sugar syrup, orange peel, Grand Marnier, vanilla bean scrapings, cinnamon stick, and nutmeg. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
6. Stir in cranberries and reduce the heat to simmer for 3 to 5 minutes, or until the berries are softened. Remove the vanilla bean and cinnamon stick from the mixture.
7. Spread the cranberry mixture evenly on the bottom of the tart shell.
8. In a separate bowl, combine pumpkin puree, brown sugar, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, cloves, salt, and white pepper. Beat in eggs, whipping cream, half and half, and bourbon until well combined.
9. Pour the pumpkin mixture into the pastry shell over the cranberry filling.
10. Bake at 375°F for 30 to 40 minutes, or until set.
11. Serve warm with cinnamon ice cream, if desired.
